free_text: Differential cross sections for the 9Be(3He,p)11B
reaction
Targets were prepared by evaporating a thin layer of
beryllium onto 10-microinch nickel foil. The targets
had thicknesses of between 25 and 50 keV to the
incident beam.
All yields were normalized to a constant amount of
integrated beam incident on the target.
Ilford C-2 200-micron nuclear emulsions for
angular distributions of the proton groups leaving the
11B nucleus in its ground and 1-st excited states
A sliding seal chamber and CsI scintillation
detector for proton groups p0 - p6 at 4.5 MeV.
The error due to normalization. Absolute cross
sections were obtained by normalizing all measurements
to an absolute differential cross-section determination
made at 2.0 MeV.
Total relative uncertainty
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
SD: Corrections in all Subents. BIB updated
The accuracy of data presentation  has been revised.
Subents 006 and 007 were merged.
The error bars include a uncertainties:
* statistical uncertainties due to the number of track
counted and
uncertainty in determination of the solid angle
uncertainty in the counting of the tracks
Points obtained using nuclear emulsion
Points obtained using a sliding seal chamber and
CsI scintillation counters

EN-SEC deleted. STATUS, ERR-ANALYS updated. ERR-DIG
added (part of the DATA-ERR). ANG-ERR -> ANG-ERR-D.
The curves were obtained by plotting, for each
bombarding energy, the weighted sum of the p0 and p1
yields at 30, 60, 90, 120 and 150 degr.
